raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that checks if contents of first file is subset of second. Use grep -F -x -f? Actually subset means all lines of first appear in second. Use grep -F -x -f file1 file2 and check exit status. So: grep -F -x -f /testbed/dir3/subdir1/subsubdir1/textfile3.txt /testbed/dir2/subdir1/textfile2.txt | wc -l? But to check subset: if all lines found, then count lines equal. So: if [ "$(grep -F -x -f file1 file2 | wc -l)" -eq "$(wc -l < file1)" ]; then echo yes; else echo no; fi. Provide one line.
